templates.name: Section_436A_Mandatory_Bail
templates.title: BAIL APPLICATION UNDER SECTION 436A CrPC
templates.applicability: When detention exceeds half of maximum sentence
templates.template:
IN THE COURT OF [MAGISTRATE/SESSIONS JUDGE]
[District/City], [State]

BAIL APPLICATION UNDER SECTION 436A CrPC

Applicant: [NAME OF ACCUSED]
           S/O [FATHER'S NAME]
           Age: [AGE], Occupation: [OCCUPATION]
           Resident of: [ADDRESS]
           
Versus

RESPONDENT: THE STATE OF [STATE]

Through: [COUNSEL NAME], Advocate

---

GROUNDS FOR BAIL:

1. The applicant has been detained in judicial custody for [NUMBER] days.

2. The maximum sentence prescribed for the offence is [NUMBER] days ([MAXIMUM SENTENCE]).

3. Pursuant to Section 436A CrPC, the applicant is entitled to MANDATORY BAIL as the detention period has exceeded half of the maximum sentence.

4. The applicant is not a flight risk and has strong community ties.

5. The applicant has cooperated with the investigation.

6. The applicant is willing to furnish bail bonds as per Court's directions.

---

LEGAL PROVISIONS:

Section 436A CrPC specifically states that after detention for half the maximum sentence period, the accused shall be released on bail as a matter of RIGHT, not discretion.

---

RELIEF SOUGHT:

It is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

1. Grant BAIL to the applicant under Section 436A CrPC.

2. Direct the applicant to furnish personal bond as per Court's directions.

3. Pass such other and further orders as this Hon'ble Court deems fit and proper.

Dated: [DATE]

                                        [SIGNATURE]
                                        [NAME OF COUNSEL]
                                        Advocate for Applicant

templates.name: Section_167_Default_Bail
templates.title: BAIL APPLICATION UNDER SECTION 167 CrPC (DEFAULT BAIL)
templates.applicability: When chargesheet not filed within prescribed time
templates.template:
IN THE COURT OF [MAGISTRATE]
[District/City], [State]

BAIL APPLICATION UNDER SECTION 167 CrPC

Applicant: [NAME OF ACCUSED]
           S/O [FATHER'S NAME]
           Age: [AGE], Occupation: [OCCUPATION]
           Resident of: [ADDRESS]
           
Versus

RESPONDENT: THE STATE OF [STATE]

Through: [COUNSEL NAME], Advocate

---

GROUNDS FOR BAIL:

1. The applicant was arrested on [DATE OF ARREST].

2. The chargesheet has NOT been filed till today ([CURRENT DATE]).

3. [NUMBER] days have elapsed since arrest, which exceeds the prescribed limit of:
   - 60 days for normal offences
   - 90 days for serious offences

4. As per Section 167 CrPC, the applicant is entitled to DEFAULT BAIL automatically.

5. The applicant is a bona fide resident of [LOCATION] with family ties.

6. There is no apprehension that the applicant will flee.

---

LEGAL BASIS:

The right to DEFAULT BAIL is a constitutional safeguard to ensure speedy trial. The Supreme Court has held that after the prescribed period, detention becomes illegal.

---

RELIEF SOUGHT:

It is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

1. Grant DEFAULT BAIL to the applicant under Section 167 CrPC.

2. Direct release of the applicant on furnishing personal bond.

3. Pass such other orders as this Hon'ble Court deems fit.

Dated: [DATE]

                                        [SIGNATURE]
                                        [NAME OF COUNSEL]
                                        Advocate for Applicant

templates.name: Section_436_Bailable_Offence
templates.title: BAIL APPLICATION FOR BAILABLE OFFENCE (SECTION 436)
templates.applicability: For offences with maximum punishment up to 3 years
templates.template:
IN THE COURT OF [MAGISTRATE]
[District/City], [State]

BAIL APPLICATION UNDER SECTION 436 CrPC

Applicant: [NAME OF ACCUSED]
           S/O [FATHER'S NAME]
           Age: [AGE], Occupation: [OCCUPATION]
           Resident of: [ADDRESS]
           
Versus

RESPONDENT: THE STATE OF [STATE]

Through: [COUNSEL NAME], Advocate

---

GROUNDS FOR BAIL:

1. The applicant is accused of [OFFENCE] which is a BAILABLE OFFENCE.

2. Section [SECTION NUMBER] IPC prescribes a maximum punishment of [TERM], which makes it bailable.

3. The applicant is entitled to bail AS OF RIGHT under Section 436 CrPC.

4. The applicant has no criminal history and is a law-abiding citizen.

5. The applicant is gainfully employed at [PLACE OF WORK].

6. The applicant has strong roots in the community and will not abscond.

7. The applicant is willing to furnish bail bonds.

---

RELEVANT PRECEDENTS:

The Supreme Court has consistently held that bail is the rule and jail is the exception, especially for bailable offences.

---

RELIEF SOUGHT:

It is most respectfully prayed that this Hon'ble Court be pleased to:

1. Grant BAIL to the applicant under Section 436 CrPC.

2. Direct the applicant to furnish personal bond of Rs. [AMOUNT].

3. Release the applicant forthwith.

Dated: [DATE]

                                        [SIGNATURE]
                                        [NAME OF COUNSEL]
                                        Advocate for Applicant
